why: Vercel currently supports 24.x as the default Node.js LTS runtime and still supports 22.x/20.x, so the project should stop pinning deploys to 22.x when moving forward.
Node 24 LTS provides a longer support window than Node 22 and avoids accidental deploys on older runtime settings.
The project remains No-AI and JSON-fallback friendly; this batch only updates runtime alignment and validation, not product scope.
honestLimitations: Running these commands on Node 22 can still pass because .npmrc keeps engine-strict=false, but that is not proof of Node 24 runtime behavior.
Hosted Vercel proof still requires a real APP_URL/NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL smoke run after deployment.
This does not claim production-ready status.
